title: centaurea amara subsp. serotina
intro: Centaurea amara subsp. serotina, also known as Late knapweed or Black knapweed, is a biennial plant within the Centaurea genus, characterized by its dark green leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. Consuming Centaurea amara subsp. serotina is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and alleviating symptoms of gastrointestinal issues. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
